metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the CI `require-ticket` job's ticket-store mount commands to use the proven `reconcile-bridge.yml` pattern: `git fetch origin '+tickets:refs/remotes/origin/tickets' --depth=1` then `git worktree add -B tickets .tickets-tracker origin/tickets` (and set `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=.tickets-tracker` or equivalent so `rebar verify-commit-ticket` resolves the store to the mounted worktree).
findings.finding: The CI `require-ticket` job's git commands for mounting the tickets store are broken as specified: `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets` with no refspec mapping does not create a local ref, so the subsequent `git worktree add .tickets-tracker` has no branch or commit to check out. The correct idiom (already used by reconcile-bridge.yml) is `git fetch origin '+tickets:refs/remotes/origin/tickets' --depth=1` followed by `git worktree add -B tickets .tickets-tracker origin/tickets`.
findings.location: AC5 (CI: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` `require-ticket` job) — the store-mounting command sequence
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the store-mount step in the `require-ticket` job with:

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the plan's `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets → git worktree add .tickets-tracker` sequence with the established prior-art pattern from `reconcile-bridge.yml`: `git fetch origin '+tickets:refs/remotes/origin/tickets' --depth=1` then `git worktree add -B tickets .tickets-tracker origin/tickets`, and document why the `origin` remote is authoritative in the Gerrit-verify context (Gerrit checkout action configures it).
findings.finding: The CI `require-ticket` job's store-mounting sequence (`git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets → git worktree add .tickets-tracker`) diverges from — and is less complete than — the established working pattern already used in `reconcile-bridge.yml` and `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` without justification. A bare `git fetch <url> tickets` only populates `FETCH_HEAD` (no named remote-tracking ref), so the subsequent `git worktree add .tickets-tracker` has no commit-ish to check out, making the sequence incomplete/broken. The prior art in this repo (`git fetch origin '+tickets:refs/remotes/origin/tickets' --depth=1` + `git worktree add -B tickets .tickets-tracker origin/tickets`) is proven and directly reusable.
findings.location: AC — CI: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` gains a `require-ticket` job … `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets → git worktree add .tickets-tracker`
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC's store-mount sequence with the exact pattern from `reconcile-bridge.yml` lines 101-104:

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the CI `require-ticket` job's store-mount commands to match the established working pattern: `git fetch origin '+tickets:refs/remotes/origin/tickets' --depth=1` + `rm -rf .tickets-tracker` + `git worktree add -B tickets .tickets-tracker origin/tickets` (mirroring `reconcile-bridge.yml:102-104`).
findings.finding: The CI `require-ticket` job's git commands for mounting the tickets store are broken as specified: `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets` without a refspec mapping leaves the branch only in FETCH_HEAD (no named tracking ref), so any subsequent `git worktree add .tickets-tracker` has no branch to point at and will fail. The established, working pattern — used identically in both `reconcile-bridge.yml` and `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` — is `git fetch origin '+tickets:refs/remotes/origin/tickets' --depth=1` + `rm -rf .tickets-tracker` + `git worktree add -B tickets .tickets-tracker origin/tickets`.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (CI `require-ticket` job): `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets → git worktree add .tickets-tracker`
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the plan's fetch+worktree spec with:

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the three unenumerated consumers: add `verify-commit-ticket` to a dispatch frozenset in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (or the `individual` set in `test_help_overview_coverage.py`), create 6 golden files in `tests/golden/cli_help/`, and update `src/rebar/_cli/help/overview.txt` to list the new subcommand.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate three unenumerated consumers that must be updated when any new subcommand is registered: (1) `tests/golden/cli_help/` needs 6 new golden files (`verify-commit-ticket__dashhelp.{out,err,exit}` and `verify-commit-ticket__help.{out,err,exit}`), (2) `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_help_overview_coverage.py` will fail `test_routable_set_matches_pinned_help_set` unless the new arm appears in a known routing frozenset or in `_routable_subcommands()`'s `individual` set, and (3) `src/rebar/_cli/help/overview.txt` must list `verify-commit-ticket` for `test_every_known_subcommand_listed_in_overview` to pass.
findings.location: AC2 — CLI intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC4's override list: remove `sync.remote` (which does not exist in `SyncConfig` or `_SECTIONS['sync']`) and replace with the actual overrides (`REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `tracker.dir` / `tracker.branch`) — or, if a remote override is genuinely desired, add `remote` to `SyncConfig` and `_SECTIONS['sync']` explicitly as a new deliverable.
findings.finding: The plan's AC4 claims store resolution is 'overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `sync.remote` / `tracker.branch`', but `sync.remote` does not exist anywhere in the codebase — `SyncConfig` has only `push` and `pull` fields, and `_SECTIONS['sync']` has no `remote` key.
findings.location: AC4 — 'Store resolution uses the standard config helpers and is overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `sync.remote` / `tracker.branch`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `sync.remote` / `tracker.branch`' with 'overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `tracker.dir` / `tracker.branch` — the standard config helpers (`config.tracker_dir()` / `config.tickets_branch()`)' since those are the actual existing paths.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC4's override chain: replace `sync.remote` with the actual config path that controls the tickets remote (currently only `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` env and `tracker.dir`/`tracker.branch` exist), or explicitly add `sync.remote` to `SyncConfig` and `_SECTIONS['sync']` as a new config key this plan introduces.
findings.finding: `sync.remote` is referenced in AC4 ('overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `sync.remote` / `tracker.branch`') as a config override path for store resolution, but `sync.remote` does not exist in `SyncConfig` or `_SECTIONS['sync']` — `SyncConfig` has only `push` and `pull`.
findings.location: AC4 — 'Store resolution uses the standard config helpers and is overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `sync.remote` / `tracker.branch`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify or remove `sync.remote` from the AC4 override chain: either define `sync.remote` as a new `SyncConfig` field (with coercer in `_SECTIONS['sync']` and an env override name) or replace it with the existing `tracker.dir` / `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` path that already achieves the same effect.
findings.finding: `sync.remote` is referenced in AC4 as part of the store-resolution override chain ('overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `sync.remote` / `tracker.branch`') but `sync.remote` does not exist in the codebase: `SyncConfig` only has `push` and `pull` fields, and `_SECTIONS['sync']` has no `remote` key. The plan neither defines this field nor specifies how it should be added.
findings.location: AC4: 'Store resolution uses the standard config helpers and is overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `sync.remote` / `tracker.branch`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'REBAR_TRACKER_DIR / sync.remote / tracker.branch' with 'REBAR_TRACKER_DIR / tracker.dir / tracker.branch' — these fields already exist in `TrackerConfig` and are the standard store-path override mechanism.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt` as an explicit AC deliverable (not merely 'help in `_help.py`') — `_help.py` is a loader, and the actual help content must be a `.txt` file in the `help/` package for `--help` routing to work.
findings.finding: The plan directs 'help in `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py`' for `verify-commit-ticket`, but `_help.py` is a pure loader — it reads `.txt` files from `src/rebar/_cli/help/`. No `verify-commit-ticket.txt` file exists there, and the CLI's `known_subcommands()` is derived exclusively from which `.txt` files are present, so without a `verify-commit-ticket.txt` the subcommand will not be recognised by the help routing and `rebar verify-commit-ticket --help` will error.
findings.location: AC2 — 'CLI `rebar verify-commit-ticket` … intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (+ help in `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Create `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt` with the usage text (reusing the shared expected-format constant); register the constant in the `.txt` template so it is the single source of truth as stated.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the help edit target: add `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt` (a NEW package-data file) as the help text artifact — do not edit `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py` for this purpose.
findings.finding: The plan directs 'help in `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py`' for `verify-commit-ticket`, but `_help.py` contains NO per-subcommand help text — it loads text from package-data files under `src/rebar/_cli/help/<subcommand>.txt`. The actual edit target for subcommand help is a NEW file `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt`, not `_help.py` itself.
findings.location: AC2 — 'intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (+ help in `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt` containing the subcommand usage text; also update `src/rebar/_cli/help/overview.txt` to list `verify-commit-ticket` in the subcommand index.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC2 to specify that help text lives in a new file `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt` (not in `_help.py`) — `_help.py` is a loader that reads from that directory; without the `.txt` file the subcommand is gated out as unknown.
findings.finding: AC2 directs 'help in `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py`' but `_help.py` is a pure loader that reads pre-authored `.txt` files from `src/rebar/_cli/help/`. Adding help text requires creating `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt`, not editing `_help.py`. The plan's instruction misdirects the implementer to the wrong file; without the `.txt` file, `known_subcommands()` will not include `verify-commit-ticket` and the subcommand will be rejected as unknown before `_dispatch()` is reached.
findings.location: AC2: 'CLI `rebar verify-commit-ticket` … intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (+ help in `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change '+ help in `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py`' to '+ add `src/rebar/_cli/help/verify-commit-ticket.txt` (the help text `_help.py` loads from `importlib.resources`)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for the `git fetch tickets` step in the CI job: distinguish fetch failure (infra, emit a clear infra-failure message and consider exiting with a neutral/infrastructure-failure code) from ticket-not-found (user error, emit the actionable diagnostic), and add a timeout so a hung fetch does not block the gate indefinitely.
findings.finding: The CI `require-ticket` job fetches the tickets branch from GitHub with no error handling, timeout, or fallback. If the fetch fails (network partition, auth failure, branch temporarily missing), the job exits non-zero in a way that is indistinguishable from a missing ticket, and the Verified gate blocks ALL commits to main with no graceful-degradation path.
findings.location: CI: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` — `require-ticket` job, `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets`
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the fetch in a step with `timeout-minutes: 2` and check its exit code explicitly: on fetch failure, echo a distinguishable infra-error message (e.g. 'ERROR: could not fetch tickets branch — infrastructure issue, not a commit problem') and exit with a code that maps to a neutral Verified vote (or at minimum surfaces a clear diagnostic separate from the missing-ticket path).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the worktree setup step idempotent in `gerrit-verify.yaml`: either prefix with `rm -rf .tickets-tracker && git worktree prune` or guard with `if [ ! -d .tickets-tracker ]` to avoid 'already exists' failures on re-runs.
findings.finding: The `git worktree add .tickets-tracker` command in the `require-ticket` CI job is not idempotent: on a re-run of the same runner workspace, or if a prior step leaves the directory behind, the command exits non-zero ('already exists' / 'already registered'), silently failing the check for the wrong reason.
findings.location: AC: CI `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` — `require-ticket` job, worktree setup step
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a cleanup step before the worktree creation:
```yaml
- name: Prepare tickets worktree
  run: |
    git worktree prune
    rm -rf .tickets-tracker
    git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets
    git worktree add .tickets-tracker FETCH_HEAD
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the explicit behavior (exit code + diagnostic) when the ticket store is unreachable or `tracker_dir` is absent, and document whether this is fail-open (exit 0 with a warning) or fail-closed (exit 1); add a corresponding test case and a CI fallback/retry strategy if fail-closed is chosen.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the command's behavior when the ticket store is unavailable at resolution time (e.g., the `git fetch … tickets` step in CI fails, the worktree is missing, or `tracker_dir` does not exist on disk). For a hard-enforcement gate this is a critical non-happy path: fail-open silently passes every commit, fail-closed blocks all commits until infra is restored — a deliberate policy choice that belongs in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — CLI AC bullet (exit 0/1 behavior) and CI AC bullet (git fetch → worktree)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: 'If `tracker_dir` cannot be resolved or the store fetch fails, exit 1 with a diagnostic naming the store path/remote and the suggested fix (run `git fetch <remote> tickets` or set `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`). This is fail-closed: infra errors do not silently pass commits.' Then add a `pytest.raises` / fixture test for a missing store, and guard the CI `require-ticket` job with `continue-on-error: false` plus a clear step name.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a unit test for the 'ticket ID extracted but not found in store' path (e.g., `rebar-ticket: NOTREAL-999` against the fixture store) asserting exit 1 and that the diagnostic shows the tried candidate(s) rather than '(none present)'.
findings.finding: The test plan covers 'no ticket present → exit 1' and 'valid ticket resolves → exit 0' but does not explicitly test the case where a syntactically valid ticket ID IS present in the commit message but fails to resolve against the store (e.g., a mistyped or nonexistent ID like `rebar-ticket: PROJ-9999`). This is a distinct user-facing failure path — the error message and exit code for 'candidate tried but not found' should be tested separately from 'no candidate extracted at all'.
findings.location: AC: Tests `tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py`
findings.suggested_fix: In `tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py`, add a parametrized case: message contains a syntactically valid trailer like `rebar-ticket: GHOST-42`, the fixture store has no such ticket, assert exit code 1 and that the diagnostic output contains the string `GHOST-42` (i.e., shows what was tried) and does NOT say '(none present)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `extract_ticket_refs` normalizes and validates each extracted candidate against a whitelist (e.g., alphanumeric, hyphens, dots) or rejects any candidate containing `/`, `..`, null bytes, or other traversal/injection characters BEFORE passing it to `_seam.require_id` / `resolve_ticket_id`, explicitly documenting this as a trust-boundary input-validation step in the AC.
findings.finding: The CI `require-ticket` job passes extracted ticket-ID candidates (taken verbatim from the untrusted Gerrit commit message) to `_seam.require_id` / `resolve_ticket_id`, which performs a filesystem lookup (`tracker / ticket_id`). The plan does not specify that extracted candidates are sanitized or validated against path-traversal sequences (e.g., `../../`) before the lookup. A crafted `rebar-ticket: ../../etc/passwd` or similar could cause the resolver to traverse outside the ticket store.
findings.location: AC: `verify_commit_message` / `extract_ticket_refs` — `src/rebar/_commands/verify_commit.py`; AC: store resolution via `_seam.require_id`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a validation step in `verify_commit_message` (or within `extract_ticket_refs`) that filters extracted candidates to only those matching a safe ID pattern (e.g., `^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_\-\.]{0,127}$` or the known rebar/Jira ID formats) before passing them to the resolver. Candidates that fail this check should be treated as 'not a ticket ID' and skipped (same as non-resolving). Document this guard explicitly in the AC as a security input-validation requirement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the CLAUDE.md edit target: since no 'git-workflow' section exists, specify that a NEW section (e.g. `## Commit conventions`) is to be CREATED in CLAUDE.md, and identify the insertion point (e.g. after the 'Gate protocols' section).
findings.finding: The plan says to update the 'CLAUDE.md git-workflow' section, but no such section heading exists in CLAUDE.md. The file has no 'git workflow', 'git-workflow', or 'Gerrit' section heading — the named edit target section is hallucinated.
findings.location: AC7 (Docs) — 'CLAUDE.md git-workflow — the same, for agents/contributors working in-repo'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'CLAUDE.md git-workflow' with 'CLAUDE.md — add a new `## Commit conventions` (or `## Git workflow`) section after the Gate protocols block'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md and CONTRIBUTING.md will stay synchronized with the Python format constant (e.g., a doctest or inline reference that imports/quotes the constant, a test that asserts doc content matches the constant, or an explicit note in the dev process).
findings.finding: The plan designates a shared Python constant as the single source of truth for the expected-format wording (used by CLI error message and --help), but does not specify that docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md and CONTRIBUTING.md will be generated from or formally linked to that constant — leaving the markdown docs free to drift from the actual extraction/resolution behavior if the format later changes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — CLI AC bullet ("The exact expected-format text lives in ONE constant reused by the error and the `--help`/docs") and Docs AC bullet (docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a unit test that imports the shared format constant and asserts that `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` contains its text verbatim (or a canonical subset). This makes doc drift a CI failure rather than a manual discipline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add AC entries for invalid-input error paths: `--rev` that fails to resolve should exit 1 with a message naming the bad ref; `--message-file` pointing to a missing file should exit 1 with the path and a fix hint; include at least one unit test for each.
findings.finding: The plan specifies three input modes (`--rev`, `--message-file`, `--message`) but does not address what happens when the supplied value is invalid: a `--rev` that does not resolve in the local repo, or a `--message-file` path that does not exist. These are common user errors and the diagnostic quality requirement ('ACTIONABLE diagnostic') should extend to them.
findings.location:
Acceptance Criteria — CLI AC bullet: `rebar verify-commit-ticket [--rev | --message-file

| --message ]`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a manual or automated end-to-end verification step to the Verification section that pushes (or simulates via `act` / a dry-run Gerrit upload) a commit missing a ticket ref and confirms the resulting Verified score is -1, or explicitly defer this to a named follow-up with a rationale.
findings.finding: The Verification section contains no end-to-end proving command that exercises the full Gerrit enforcement path — i.e., pushing a commit without a ticket ref to Gerrit and observing a Verified-1 vote. The two local CLI commands (items 2 and 3) exercise the `rebar` binary in isolation; the unit tests are offline. The critical production behavior (Gerrit Verified-1 blocks submit) is unproven by any described step.
findings.location: Verification section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'already in use' framing: the `rebar-ticket:` trailer is a NEW convention being established by this plan — update the docs AC (CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md`) to introduce it as new, not merely reinforce it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the `rebar-ticket:` commit trailer 'already exists' and is 'already in use', implying it is an established convention in this repo. Searching all source, docs, CONTRIBUTING.md, and CLAUDE.md yields zero occurrences of the trailer in any actual commit message, documentation, or workflow guidance.
findings.location: Context / Why — '`rebar-ticket:` commit trailer already exists but nothing parses it'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly declare in the CI AC how the `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets` step is authenticated (e.g., `persist-credentials: true` on the checkout, or explicit `GITHUB_TOKEN` injection), mirroring the pattern used in `reconcile-bridge.yml`, and note whether the fetch works anonymously for public repos vs. requires credentials for private repos.
findings.finding: The CI `require-ticket` job plans to run `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets` inside the `build-and-test` job (which uses `checkout-gerrit-change-action` without `persist-credentials: true`), but the plan does not declare whether this fetch requires authentication (for a private repository) or how credentials are made available. The existing pattern in the codebase for the same fetch (e.g., `reconcile-bridge.yml`) explicitly requires `persist-credentials: true`; its absence on the Gerrit verify job is a potential gap at lower severity.
findings.location: AC: CI job — `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`, `require-ticket` job step `git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add distinguishable, structured diagnostic output (at minimum: different exit messages / stderr prefixes) for 'store fetch/access failure' vs 'ticket not found', so operators and CI dashboards can distinguish infrastructure failures from user errors without reading full logs.
findings.finding: The plan adds no observability instrumentation for the new failure point: there is no metric, structured log, or alert that distinguishes 'tickets store unreachable' from 'ticket genuinely not found', making it impossible for operators to know whether failures are user errors or infrastructure problems without reading individual CI logs.
findings.location: AC: `verify_commit_message` resolution logic; CI `require-ticket` job

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `env: REBAR_TRACKER_DIR: .tickets-tracker` (or equivalent `rebar.toml` override) to the `require-ticket` CI job spec so the store-resolution path is unambiguously wired to the fetched worktree.
findings.finding: The CI job description instructs creating a worktree at `.tickets-tracker` for the tickets store but never explicitly sets `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=.tickets-tracker` (or equivalent config) so that `rebar verify-commit-ticket --rev HEAD` actually resolves tickets against that worktree. An executor following the plan literally could create the worktree but leave the env var unset, causing the command to fall back to the default tracker_dir and silently skip resolution or error.
findings.location: CI AC bullet — `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` `require-ticket` job description
findings.suggested_fix: In the CI AC bullet, extend the job description to include: `env:\n  REBAR_TRACKER_DIR: .tickets-tracker` on the `rebar verify-commit-ticket --rev HEAD` step, and add a Verification item: `echo $REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / inspect the job log to confirm the env is set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step in the CI AC showing how the `.tickets-tracker` worktree is wired to the resolver — either by setting `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=$(pwd)/.tickets-tracker` in the CI job environment, or by confirming that the standard config (`tracker_dir`) in `rebar.toml` already points to a path that the worktree satisfies.
findings.finding: The CI job section describes fetching the tickets store and creating a worktree at `.tickets-tracker`, but never specifies that `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` (or equivalent config) must be set to point the resolver at that worktree. The Store resolution AC separately states resolution is overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`. Without an explicit link, the CI step sets up the store but the resolver has no guaranteed way to find it — the implementer must infer the connection.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — CI job AC vs Store resolution AC
findings.suggested_fix: In the CI AC, after the `git worktree add .tickets-tracker` step, add: `env: REBAR_TRACKER_DIR: ${{ github.workspace }}/.tickets-tracker` on the `rebar verify-commit-ticket` run step, and note in the Store resolution AC that CI overrides via this env var.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure to the plan (e.g. 'to disable enforcement: set `verify.require_ticket_for_commit = false` in `rebar.toml` and push, or revert the workflow YAML; no data cleanup required') and consider a short observation period (e.g. shadow-mode / log-only run) before enabling the flag in `rebar.toml`.
findings.finding: The rollback procedure is not explicitly stated. Although the feature flag `verify.require_ticket_for_commit` defaults to `false` and provides an implicit rollback path, the plan enables it for this project in the same changeset that introduces enforcement — there is no described canary or observation period before full enforcement, and the explicit rollback steps (flip flag in rebar.toml + push, or revert the workflow YAML) are not documented.
findings.location: AC: Config section — 'enabled for THIS project in `rebar.toml`'; Rollout/Rollback section (absent)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' note to the plan: 'Rollback: flip `verify.require_ticket_for_commit` to `false` in `rebar.toml` (or set `REBAR_VERIFY_REQUIRE_TICKET_FOR_COMMIT=false` in CI env) — takes effect on next push, no data cleanup. Optionally run in log-only mode for one sprint before full enforcement.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `permissions: contents: read` (minimum needed for `git fetch`) to the `require-ticket` job in `gerrit-verify.yaml` so the GITHUB_TOKEN scope is pinned and not subject to org-default drift.
findings.finding: The `require-ticket` job specification does not define an explicit `permissions:` block, so the job inherits the workflow-level (or org-default) GITHUB_TOKEN permissions, which may be overly broad (e.g., `write-all` if the org default is permissive) or may lack `contents: read` if the org enforces restrictive defaults, causing the `git fetch` to fail silently.
findings.location: AC: CI `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` — `require-ticket` job definition
findings.suggested_fix:
```yaml
require-ticket:
  permissions:
    contents: read
  needs: clear-vote
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    ...
```

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
